_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron, 1450 batch)
_meta.note: RVA city batch — 5 Richmond, VA officials (City of Richmond): danny-avula (Mayor, sworn 1/1/2025 — 81st Mayor), colette-mceachin (Commonwealth's Attorney, D, re-elected unopposed Nov 2025 after 71% Dem primary win), anne-holton (RPS School Board D6, interim appt 2/3/2026 7-0-1 to fill Harris-Muhammed seat; running Nov 2026 special), cynthia-newbille (Council D7 / President 2025-2026, served since 2009), andrew-breton (Council D1 'Gumby' Breton, sworn 1/1/2025, former chair RVA City Democratic Committee). KEY EVIDENCE: (1) Avula = evangelical Christian (East End Fellowship, NOT Catholic — corrects my prior brief); openly broke with church tradition on abortion + LGBTQ; ran TV ad featuring his gay daughter; hosted 2025 + 2026 Pride flag raisings + signed joint Pride proclamation 'Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community in perpetuity'; HARD FALSE econ_q2 — Oct 2025 actively lobbied Council to REJECT a 4-cent property-tax cut, quote 'Now's not the time to cut Richmond's property taxes'; HARD FALSE life_q0/q1 — pledged to 'preserve and protect Richmonders' reproductive freedom—including access to abortion and contraception' and continue city support for Planned Parenthood; HARD FALSE pj_q3 — created Office of Gun Violence Prevention with root-causes/public-health framing (he's an MD/MPH); HARD FALSE border_q1/q2 — May 2026 announced 4 measures to wall off RPD from ICE incl Flock LPR data cutoff, quote 'Richmond does not and will not coordinate with ICE on deportation'; HARD FALSE 2A_q1 — pro-gun-control posture, signed Democratic Mayors op-ed 'Grief is not enough,' supported HB110/SB496 locked-storage. (2) McEachin = veteran D prosecutor (in office 25+ years before 2019 win), Brown undergrad + UVA Law (NOT W&M as I guessed), widow of late Rep. Donald McEachin (AME minister) — Colette is institutionally Baptist (St. Peter Baptist CDC board) not AME; NOT a Soros DA (declined to join VA Progressive Prosecutors for Justice, was attacked from her left by Soros-aligned Tom Barbour in 2025 primary which she won 71%; ran unopposed Nov 2025 general); HARD TRUE elec_q4 — Oct 2025 personally announced 4-count Class-5 felony election-fraud indictment of Tavares Floyd after 10-month VSP probe; HARD FALSE pj_q1 — virtually eliminated cash bond system, diversion programs, Behavioral Health Docket, expungement expansion; HARD FALSE pj_q3 — 'Community Justice Reform Unit to tackle root causes of crime'; HARD FALSE border_q1/q2 — office statement 'We do not work with any local, state or federal law enforcement agency to detain or arrest anyone based solely upon their immigration status,' McEachin quote 'ICE cannot just barge into the Richmond Circuit Court courthouse,' personally distributes 'blue cards' explaining rights to refuse searches. NOT scored: 2020 Peters ruling (justified) — judicial determination, NOT advocacy, doesn't clear HIGH BAR for pj_q0 per skill; gun-violence prosecution rhetoric ('you are making Richmond dangerous because you have a gun') is anti-gun framing but doesn't specifically map to red-flag/AWB rubric so 2A_q1 left null. (3) Anne Holton = Catholic at St. Elizabeth Richmond (NOT Episcopalian as I guessed), Princeton + Harvard Law (NOT W&M/UVA), interim-appointed 2/3/2026 to replace resigned Shonda Harris-Muhammed, running Nov 2026 special; former VA SecEd under McAuliffe (2014-2016) + VA BOE member Feb 2017-~2024; HARD FALSE family_q0 — pushed back against charter expansion as VA SecEd, lone NO on Roanoke College lab school 2024 ('lowering the standards… just to get it done fast'); HARD FALSE family_q2 — objected to Youngkin draft history standards as VA BOE member, called November 2022 draft 'a disaster,' noted Reagan-5x-vs-Obama-0x asymmetry; SOFT FALSE family_q1 + bib_marriage_q4 + refuse_overreach_q0 — Aug 29 2024 X thread + Blue Virginia: called Youngkin BOE policy changes 'terrible changes that are bad for VA students, parents, and educators' (the policies she opposed were the parental-notification + biological-sex + LGBTQ-rollback model policies, so her opposition is HARD-direction adverse on the rubric cells those policies addressed); HARD TRUE econ_q2 — Mar 2-3 2026 RPS FY27 budget she + VP Percival cast the two 'no' votes in 6-2 board approval of ~$549.9M Avula package, arguing 'unrealistic' given coming city property-tax freeze; also voted against funding Richmond Virtual Academy. HARD FALSE 2A_q0/q1 — campaign on 'tighter restrictions on guns,' citing 'over 50 RPS students lost to guns' in seven years (Richmonder special-election piece). Most other cells null (not in school-board lane). (4) Newbille = Council President 2025-2026 (3rd stint), public-health PhD; HARD FALSE bib_marriage_q4 — co-signed June 10 2025 Joint Proclamation 'Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community in perpetuity' as Council President; HARD FALSE econ_q2 — Oct 14-15 2025 co-sponsored with Mayor Avula proposal to HOLD real-estate tax rate steady at $1.20/$100, defeated Trammell tax-cut, quote 'I do not see [tax-rate reduction] in this moment'; HARD TRUE pj_q0 — in 2020 voted with majority REJECTING police-budget cuts during defund moment, in 2022 affirmatively proposed RPD + fire pay raises ($1.9M-$4M, with VP Robertson and Trammell), in Feb 2026 walked Shockoe Bottom businesses with Chief Edwards after mass shooting ('share, hear, map out strategies') — multi-vector affirmative pro-police, clears HIGH BAR; HARD FALSE pj_q3 — May 2021 unanimous council declaration 'gun violence is a public health crisis' (Newbille is the council's leading public-health-policy PhD, the quintessential q3 fit); HARD FALSE refuse_overreach_q2 — Sept 13 2021 unanimous Climate and Ecological Emergency Resolution = HARD-direction ESG embrace (rubric q2 specifically mentions ESG); HARD FALSE border_q2 — co-signed admin statement 'Local government is not in charge of federal immigration law enforcement and Richmond does not detain or arrest anyone due to immigration status' (RPD has not signed 287(g)); HARD FALSE 2A_q0/q1 — July 1 2019 supported ordinance banning firearms in Richmond city buildings/parks, quote 'one way to limit gun violence.' Hard-anti-defund + Hard-pro-funding-RPD via pay-raise proposal is a meaningful counter-archetype softener vs simple Democratic-progressive coding — captured in the TRUE pj_q0 score. (5) Breton = 'Gumby' Breton (former chair RVA City Democratic Committee, RPS public-school dad / former PTA fundraising chair), sworn 1/1/2025 first term to 12/31/2028; deliberately silent on culture-war markers in public materials — most cells null per skill discipline; HARD FALSE pj_q3 — Richmond Mayorathon answer 'Funding resources and interventions for those who are most at-risk to commit violence will reduce incidents' is textbook public-health/intervention framing of violence; HARD FALSE 2A_q0/q1 — organized June 7 2023 children's march on VA State Capitol for gun-safety legislation in wake of Huguenot HS graduation shooting, listed as Moms Demand Action endorsee on campaign endorsements page. Sparse cited cells (3 FALSE / 0 TRUE) reflect his deliberate culture-war silence in public materials — honest report per the 'NO EVIDENCE FOUND beats inference' brand discipline. RICK-WEST FLAG (carrying forward from 1625 dossier): rick-west (VA Mayor of Chesapeake, R) was first by priority but remains slug-collided with rick-west (OK State Rep, R, archetype_party_default) — engine keys by slug not (state,slug). Skipped again this batch; needs a slug-rename helper script (proposed: rick-west-chesapeake) before scoring.

profile.religion: Evangelical Christian — founding member of East End Fellowship, a multi-ethnic evangelical church in Church Hill, Richmond (NOT Catholic). Public bio at https://www.theologicalhorizons.org/events1/dannyavula confirms. Richmonder.org (campaign) flagged his 'Christian faith and affiliation with a church that's taken conservative stances on marriage and abortion' as a campaign issue — which Avula countered by publicly affirming LGBTQ equality and abortion rights.
profile.education: B.A. Biology, University of Virginia (1999); M.D., Virginia Commonwealth University; M.P.H., Johns Hopkins.
profile.background: Born 1978 in Hyderabad, India; immigrated as a child — first Asian-American and first immigrant Mayor of Richmond. Pediatrician by training. Career: Director, Richmond/Henrico Health Districts (6 yrs); Virginia State Vaccination Coordinator (Jan 2021 – early 2022, appointed by Gov. Northam); Commissioner, Virginia Department of Social Services (under Northam and Youngkin). Sworn in as 81st Mayor of Richmond Jan 1 2025; current term ends Jan 1 2029. Wife Mary Kay (RPS elementary teacher); five children including one adopted; openly gay daughter (featured in 2024 campaign ad). Self-described 'committed Democrat' on an officially-nonpartisan Richmond mayoral ballot.

danny-avula.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: 81st Mayor of Richmond, VA (sworn 1/1/2025; term 1/1/2025-1/1/2029). Officially nonpartisan ballot; real-world alignment Democrat. RELIGION CORRECTION: evangelical (East End Fellowship), NOT Catholic. EDUCATION CORRECTION: UVa undergrad + VCU MD + JHU MPH. HARD FALSE sanctity_of_life q0 + q1: campaign Priorities page pledges to 'Preserve and protect Richmonders' reproductive freedom—including access to abortion and contraception' and continue city support for Planned Parenthood; publicly supported VA League for Planned Parenthood's proposed Northside facility (VPM Oct 29 2024). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q0 + q4: ran TV ad featuring his openly gay daughter to counter pro-LGBTQ-equality challenge to his church's traditional posture; personally hosted 2025 AND 2026 Pride Flag raising ceremonies at Richmond City Hall and issued joint Mayor-Council Pride proclamation declaring Richmond 'LGBTQ+ Friendly Community in perpetuity' (May 30 2025 + 2026 cycles). Direct quote (May 30 2026): 'Richmond stands proudly with our LGBTQ community not just in policy or symbolism, but in spirit. We will not go backward.' biblical_marriage q2: pledged to 'push back against any state or federal attempts to reduce protections and rights for LGBTQ+ people' — language treats LGBTQ+ rights as a unified bundle including T; left null per strict cite-only discipline (no specific quote on trans-identity / biological sex). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: Oct 2025 Avula administration actively lobbied Council to REJECT a 4-cent real estate tax reduction (from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100), warning it would cost $17M; Council sided with Avula; CAO answered 'No ma'am' when asked if admin would budget for lower taxes. Direct Avula quote: 'Now's not the time to cut Richmond's property taxes.' FY27 budget proposed up to $3.4B-$3.5B keeping the $1.20 rate (highest in region; Chesterfield/Hanover/Henrico all under $0.90) and including utility-rate increases. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: created the Office of Gun Violence Prevention (Apr 17 2025) explicitly using root-causes / public-health framing — as an MD/MPH this is his professional default and it is on the record. NOT scored TRUE on public_justice q0 — public-safety unions (police + fire) endorsed his 2024 campaign and he has not anti-defund-quoted, but his signature crime initiative is community/root-causes-framed not affirmative-pro-cop, no FOP endorsement located, and the SKILL's HIGH BAR for q0 ('affirmative advocacy, NOT just routine collaboration') is not cleared. HARD FALSE border_immigration q1 + q2: as an immigrant himself publicly opposes deportation enforcement; in May 2026 announced 4 measures to wall off RPD from ICE — (a) cutting federal-agency access to Flock LPR data, (b) blocking data-sharing with any VA jurisdiction having a 287(g), (c) new 'RICHMOND POLICE' uniform patches to distinguish from ICE, (d) outreach. Quote: 'Richmond does not and will not coordinate with ICE on deportation.' Richmond appears on the federal sanctuary-jurisdictions list. HARD FALSE self_defense q1: co-authored Democratic Mayors op-ed 'Grief is not enough' calling for state gun-violence action; publicly supported HB110/SB496 locked-storage legislation; quoted: 'Access to guns has really been a challenge… I'm looking forward to some new legislation in that arena.' self_defense q0 left null (no explicit constitutional-carry-no-permit quote located, only directional inference). Refuse_state_overreach left null across cells — his 2021 vaccine-mandate-enabler role was state-level pre-mayoral activity (office mismatch) and his 'push back against state rollbacks' framing on LGBTQ is direction-inverted relative to the rubric (resists conservative state action, not progressive overreach), so absent a clean current-office cite the cells stay null per strict discipline rather than inferring. Christian_liberty cells null — he speaks publicly about his own faith (Feb 2026 Theological Horizons event) but has issued no defense of others' religious-liberty claims in office. Family_child_sovereignty null — Mayor does not control RPS curriculum directly; no parental-notification / school-choice / CRT statement located. Election_integrity null — Registrar is separate office. 0 TRUE / 8 FALSE on cited cells = 0% — extreme but coherent (textbook progressive Democratic Mayor with documented Christian-faith background broken on the standard culture-war markers). Recommend revisiting after FY28 budget cycle and any 2028 re-election campaign positions.

0.note: Campaign Priorities page pledges to 'Preserve and protect Richmonders' reproductive freedom—including access to abortion and contraception' and continue city support for Planned Parenthood; publicly supported Virginia League for Planned Parenthood's proposed Northside facility. Opposite of affirming life from conception.

1.note: Active advocate of expanding (not abolishing) abortion access — same Priorities page pledge, same VPM coverage.

0.note: Publicly affirms LGBTQ equality; ran 2024 TV ad featuring his openly gay daughter to counter conservative-church criticism. Opposite of affirming marriage as exclusively one man + one woman.

4.note: Personally hosted 2025 and 2026 Pride Flag raisings at Richmond City Hall; issued joint Mayor-Council Pride proclamation declaring Richmond an 'LGBTQ+ Friendly Community in perpetuity.' Direct quote May 30 2026: 'Richmond stands proudly with our LGBTQ community not just in policy or symbolism, but in spirit. We will not go backward.' Affirmative state-government promotion of LGBTQ identity — the rubric's adverse signal.

2.note: Oct 2025: Avula admin actively lobbied Council to REJECT a 4-cent real-estate tax reduction (from $1.20→$1.16/$100), warning it would cost $17M; Council sided with Avula. Avula quote: 'Now's not the time to cut Richmond's property taxes.' CAO answered 'No ma'am' when asked if admin would budget for lower taxes. FY27 budget proposed up to $3.4B-$3.5B keeping $1.20 rate (highest in metro region — Chesterfield/Hanover/Henrico all under $0.90) and including utility-rate increases. Direct, on-the-record opposition to a tax-rate reduction → adverse on the rubric's rate-restraint cell.

3.note: Apr 17 2025 announced the Office of Gun Violence Prevention explicitly addressing 'root causes' and connecting 'high-risk individuals with services' — textbook public-health framing of violent crime. As an MD/MPH this is his professional default and it is on the record. Adverse on the rubric's q3 cell.

1.note: As an immigrant himself, publicly opposes deportation enforcement and has issued multiple Mayor's-Office statements expressing concern over ICE actions in Richmond; opposite of the rubric's mandatory-deportation cell.

2.note: May 2026 announced 4 measures to further wall off RPD from ICE: (1) cutting federal-agency access to Flock LPR data, (2) blocking data-sharing with any VA jurisdiction having a 287(g), (3) new 'RICHMOND POLICE' uniform patches to distinguish from ICE, (4) outreach. Direct quote: Richmond 'does not and will not coordinate with ICE on deportation.' RPD has refused to sign a 287(g). Richmond appears on the federal sanctuary-jurisdictions list. HARD adverse to q2's federal-preemption-against-sanctuary cell.

1.note: Co-authored Democratic Mayors of America op-ed 'Grief is not enough: local leadership and state action needed to prevent gun violence' calling for state gun-violence legislation; publicly supported HB110/SB496 locked-storage legislation; quoted: 'Access to guns has really been a challenge… I'm looking forward to some new legislation in that arena.' Affirmative pro-gun-control posture — adverse to the rubric's opposes-red-flag/AWB/registries cell direction.

profile.religion: Institutionally Baptist — public bio states she previously served on the governing board of St. Peter Baptist Church Child Development Center. No public personal profession of faith in office located. Distinct from her late husband Rep. A. Donald McEachin, who was an ordained AME minister.
profile.education: B.A. Brown University; J.D. University of Virginia School of Law (per primary bio at rva.gov/commonwealths-attorney/about — note: prior brief speculation 'William & Mary undergrad' is incorrect).
profile.background: First woman and first African-American woman elected Commonwealth's Attorney for the City of Richmond. 25+ years in the Richmond CA's office before winning the 2019 special general election after predecessor Mike Herring resigned to join McGuireWoods (May 2019). Re-elected unopposed in 2021. Won the June 17 2025 Democratic primary over Tom Barbour Jr. (~71% vs ~29%) and ran unopposed in the Nov 4 2025 general; new term seated Jan 2026. Widow of the late Rep. A. Donald McEachin (VA-04), who died Nov 2022; they met in law school at UVA and have three children. Life member Delta Sigma Theta, NAACP, Richmond Crusade for Voters. Notable: she publicly DECLINED to join 'Virginia Progressive Prosecutors for Justice,' saying she 'doesn't need to join a club' to make change — her 2025 primary challenger Tom Barbour ran from her left as the explicitly-progressive challenger, asserting 'Colette McEachin is not a progressive prosecutor' (Virginia Mercury, VPM).

colette-mceachin.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Commonwealth's Attorney, City of Richmond, VA (D); new term seated Jan 2026 after winning 2025 Dem primary 71% and running unopposed Nov 2025 general. EDUCATION CORRECTION: Brown undergrad + UVA Law (not W&M / Anglican as I speculated). RELIGION CORRECTION: institutionally Baptist (St. Peter Baptist CDC board), NOT AME (her late husband was the AME minister, not her). SOROS-DA FINDING: NO evidence she received Soros / Justice & Public Safety PAC money — that money flowed to her 2025 challenger Tom Barbour. She publicly declined to join VA Progressive Prosecutors for Justice and was attacked from her left in the primary. Heritage Foundation / LELDF Soros-DA rosters do NOT list her. HARD TRUE election_integrity q4: Oct 6 2025 her office personally announced a 4-count Class-5 felony election-fraud indictment of 2024 Richmond City Council candidate Tavares Floyd after a 10-month Virginia State Police probe (campaign-finance falsification, dead 'donors') — affirmative willingness to prosecute election crime. HARD FALSE public_justice q1: continued Herring's policy of not seeking cash bail in many cases — 'virtually eliminated the cash bond system for those awaiting trial' (Virginia Mercury); diversion programs for first-time non-violent, addiction, mental-health, behavioral-health; created a 'Community Justice Reform Unit to tackle root causes of crime'; expanded expungement. Multi-vector bail-reform / soft-on-crime trigger per the rubric definition (even though she also prosecutes murders aggressively and explicitly declined the 'progressive prosecutor' label — net cited direction is FALSE on q1). HARD FALSE public_justice q3: same 'Community Justice Reform Unit to tackle root causes of crime' + Behavioral Health Docket + restorative-justice integration = public-health framing of crime (rubric-adverse). HARD FALSE border_immigration q1 + q2: office statement 'We do not work with any local, state or federal law enforcement agency to detain or arrest anyone based solely upon their immigration status'; McEachin direct quote 'ICE cannot just barge into the Richmond Circuit Court courthouse, or any of the courthouses, and just grab people'; personally distributes 'blue cards' to immigrant communities in English/Spanish explaining rights to refuse searches and access counsel; office denied FOIA request for ICE communications; her quote 'This is me saying to Trump, the Constitution still lives.' Clear anti-ICE-cooperation posture on the federal-preemption-against-sanctuary cell. NOT scored TRUE on public_justice q0: 2020 Marcus-David Peters JUSTIFIED ruling (refused to reopen despite intense activist pressure) and 2020 RPD-officer charge handling (dismissed misdemeanor assault charges after restorative-justice resolution) are evidence she is NOT a Soros-style anti-cop prosecutor — but they are prosecutorial / judicial determinations based on evidence, NOT 'affirmative advocacy' per the SKILL's HIGH BAR (no FOP endorsement, no anti-defund rally, no police-partnership program she runs). Per skill discipline q0 left null rather than sparse-data-trap TRUE. NOT scored q2 (victim restitution): campaign touts 'expanded services supporting victims of crime' and family-justice-center goal but those are victim-services language, not specifically defendant-pays-victim restitution; left null per strict cite-only discipline. NOT scored q4 (Romans 13:4 magistrate sword): her 'sacred duty' framing ('We are the scales of justice... that is a sacred duty') is generic civic-religious not Christian-explicit; left null. NOT scored self_defense q0/q1: her aggressive gun-violence prosecution ('if you use a gun in a harmful or threatening way in the city of Richmond, you will be prosecuted') and 'you are making Richmond dangerous because you have a gun' are anti-gun rhetoric but do not specifically map to the rubric's constitutional-carry / red-flag / AWB / registry cells; left null. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / economic_stewardship / refuse_state_overreach — no quote-bearing positions located. 1 TRUE / 5 FALSE on cited cells = ~17% — coherent for a center-left D prosecutor who has implemented some reformist policies (bail, restorative justice) while still aggressively prosecuting homicides, gun violence, and election fraud. Recommend revisiting on the 2026 prosecution-priorities report and any next-term policy announcements.

4.note: Oct 6 2025 her office personally announced a 4-count Class-5 felony election-fraud indictment of 2024 Richmond City Council candidate Tavares Floyd after a 10-month Virginia State Police investigation (campaign-finance falsification, dead 'donors'). Affirmative willingness to prosecute election crime is the rubric-positive direction on the election-integrity cell.

1.note: Continued Herring's policy of not seeking cash bail in many cases — 'virtually eliminated the cash bond system for those awaiting trial' per Virginia Mercury; diversion programs for first-time non-violent, addiction, mental-health, behavioral-health cases; created a 'Community Justice Reform Unit to tackle root causes of crime'; expanded expungement. Even though she declined the 'progressive prosecutor' label and aggressively prosecutes homicides and gun violence, the cited bail-elimination + diversion + expungement policies map directly to the rubric's cashless-bail / soft-on-crime trigger → FALSE.

3.note: Campaign and office materials repeatedly use the 'Community Justice Reform Unit to tackle root causes of crime' frame; Behavioral Health Docket and restorative-justice integration are foundational office programs. Public-health-of-crime framing — rubric-adverse on q3.

1.note: Office statement: 'We do not work with any local, state or federal law enforcement agency to detain or arrest anyone based solely upon their immigration status.' McEachin direct quote: 'ICE cannot just barge into the Richmond Circuit Court courthouse, or any of the courthouses, and just grab people.' Personally distributes 'blue cards' to immigrant communities explaining rights to refuse searches and access counsel. Clear anti-deportation posture.

2.note: Same anti-ICE-cooperation posture (see q1); office denied FOIA request for ICE communications, citing criminal-investigative-file exemption — withholding the very records that would prove or disprove cooperation. Clear sanctuary-aligned posture against federal preemption.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic — congregant of St. Elizabeth Catholic Church in Richmond, a predominantly African American parish; married Tim Kaine in November 1984 at a Roman Catholic church in Richmond (per Wikipedia, primary biography sources).
profile.education: Princeton University, Woodrow Wilson School of Public and International Affairs, magna cum laude (1980); J.D. Harvard Law School (1983).
profile.background: Born Feb 1 1958 in Roanoke, VA. Daughter of moderate-Republican Gov. Linwood Holton (1970-1974) — at age 12 her father enrolled her at the predominantly Black Mosby Middle School (now MLK Jr. Middle School) to end Massive Resistance to school integration, a defining biographical fact she cites constantly. Career: law clerk for Judge Robert R. Merhige Jr. (EDVA); Central Virginia Legal Aid Society attorney (1983-1998); Richmond Juvenile & Domestic Relations District Court judge (sworn 6/30/1998, chief judge 2000-2003, resigned late 2005); First Lady of VA 2006-2010 (married Sen. Tim Kaine); Virginia Secretary of Education 1/11/2014 – 7/25/2016 under Gov. Terry McAuliffe; Virginia Board of Education member Feb 2017 – ~2024 (appointed by McAuliffe); George Mason University visiting professor (May 2017) and interim president 6/20/2019 – 6/30/2020. Three adult children — Nat (b.1990), Woody (b.1992), Annella (b.1995) — all RPS graduates. Brothers Woody Holton (historian, USC) and Dwight Holton (former US Attorney, Oregon). RPS School Board, District 6 (officially nonpartisan ballot, real-world Democratic alignment) — interim-appointed Feb 3 2026 by a 7-0-1 board vote to fill the seat vacated by resigned Shonda Harris-Muhammed (Dec 2025, departed to be Southampton County superintendent); announced she will run in the Nov 2026 special election (candidacy_status set general_candidate).

anne-holton.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond Public Schools Board, District 6 (officially nonpartisan, real-world firm Democratic alignment — former VA SecEd under McAuliffe, wife of Sen. Tim Kaine, daughter of moderate-R Gov. Linwood Holton but her own career is firmly Democratic). Interim-appointed Feb 3 2026 by 7-0-1 board vote to fill the Shonda Harris-Muhammed seat; running for the seat in Nov 2026 special. RELIGION CORRECTION: Catholic at St. Elizabeth Richmond (not Episcopalian as I speculated). EDUCATION CORRECTION: Princeton + Harvard Law (not W&M / UVA). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q0: as VA Secretary of Education 2014-2016 'pushed back against the expansion of charter schools' in Virginia and advocated for teacher pay increases instead (WVTF Jul 28 2016, Wash Post); on VA Board of Education Apr 25 2024 was the lone NO vote on the Roanoke College lab school program and four others, direct quote: 'I'm concerned that, in our haste to get this done quickly, we're lowering the standards for what we've seen in the rest of lab schools, just to get it done fast.' HARD multi-vector evidence of opposition to school-choice expansion. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: on VA BOE 2022-2023 objected to Youngkin's draft history standards, called the November 2022 draft 'a disaster,' noted Reagan-5x-vs-Obama-0x asymmetry, said 'Teachers have to walk gingerly around anything that connects what happens today with the past'; joined Board President Daniel Gecker and VP Tammy Mann (Northam/McAuliffe appointees) opposing the introductory pages as 'political' (Virginia Mercury, WDBJ7). HARD-direction adverse on the CRT / standards-restriction rubric cell. SOFT FALSE biblical_marriage q4 + family_child_sovereignty q1 + refuse_state_overreach q0: Aug 29 2024 X thread (status 1829241205441311103) + Blue Virginia called the Youngkin BOE's 'changes' — which centered on parental notification, biological-sex / model-policy reversal, and conservative history standards — 'terrible changes that are bad for VA students, parents, and educators' (HARD direction-adverse statement on the policies the cited cells protect). HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: Mar 2-3 2026 RPS FY27 budget Holton and VP Matthew Percival cast the 2 NO votes in the 6-2 board approval of the ~$549.9M Avula-proposed package, arguing it was 'unrealistic' to expect additional funds with city property-tax freezes coming (VPM Mar 3 2026, Axios Richmond Mar 2 2026); also voted against funding the Richmond Virtual Academy for early-cut reasons. Affirmative fiscal-restraint vote against an oversized education budget = rubric-positive on q2 even from a Democratic education-establishment figure (a meaningful counter-archetype softener captured per skill discipline). HARD FALSE self_defense q0 + q1: campaigns on 'tighter restrictions on guns,' cites 'over 50 [RPS] students [lost] to guns' in seven years and calls the situation 'inexcusable' (The Richmonder special-election article). NOT scored sanctity_of_life / christian_liberty / election_integrity / public_justice / border_immigration — outside school-board lane and no quote-bearing positions located. NOT scored biblical_marriage q0/q2 — RPS Oct 3 2022 transgender policy rejection vote (8-1) PREDATES her tenure (she joined Feb 2026), no direct vote record; her general posture is direction-adverse but rubric strictness keeps q0/q2 null and credits q4 only because the Aug 2024 X thread specifically named the policy reversal she opposed. NOT scored family_child_sovereignty q1 cited cell despite Aug 2024 thread — promoted from null to SOFT-FALSE based on the same cite because the Youngkin model policies center on parental notification on social transition specifically. NOT scored refuse_state_overreach q1-q4: no specific quotes on COVID-mandate-refusal / ESG / subsidiarity / refused-enforcement located. 1 TRUE / 6 FALSE on cited cells = ~14% — coherent for standard Northam/McAuliffe-Democratic education establishment figure with a fiscal-caution counter-archetype softener (the FY27 NO vote). Recommend revisiting after the Nov 2026 special election outcome and any 2027 RPS culture-war votes.

4.note: Aug 29 2024 X thread + Blue Virginia coverage: as VA Board of Education member Holton publicly opposed the Youngkin BOE majority's reversal of the Northam-era LGBTQ-protective model policies, calling the changes 'terrible changes that are bad for VA students, parents, and educators.' The reversed policies were the parental-notification + biological-sex / LGBTQ-rollback model policies, so her opposition is direction-adverse on the rubric's oppose-LGBTQ-promotion cell.

0.note: As VA Secretary of Education 2014-2016 'pushed back against the expansion of charter schools' in Virginia and advocated for teacher pay increases instead. On VA Board of Education April 25 2024 was the lone NO vote on the Roanoke College lab school program and four other lab-school approvals; direct quote: 'I'm concerned that, in our haste to get this done quickly, we're lowering the standards for what we've seen in the rest of lab schools, just to get it done fast.' Multi-vector affirmative opposition to school-choice expansion.

1.note: Aug 29 2024 X thread + Blue Virginia: Holton publicly opposed the Youngkin BOE majority's policy changes, which centered on parental notification on social transition and biological-sex policies. Her opposition is direction-adverse on the parental-notification rubric cell.

2.note: On VA BOE 2022-2023 objected to Youngkin's draft history standards, called the November 2022 draft 'a disaster,' noted Reagan-5x-vs-Obama-0x asymmetry, said 'Teachers have to walk gingerly around anything that connects what happens today with the past'; joined Northam/McAuliffe-appointee colleagues opposing the introductory pages as 'political.' Direction-adverse on the rubric's oppose-CRT / oppose-restrictionist-curriculum cell.

Mar 2-3 2026 RPS FY27 budget Holton and VP Matthew Percival cast the 2 NO votes in the 6-2 board approval of the ~$549.9M Avula-proposed package, arguing it was 'unrealistic' to expect additional funds with city property-tax freezes coming. Also voted against funding the Richmond Virtual Academy line for early-cut reasons. Affirmative fiscal-restraint vote against an oversized education budget = rubric-positive on q2.

profile.education: B.A. psychology/linguistics, SUNY Stony Brook (1974); M.A. psychology, SUNY Stony Brook (1983); Ph.D. Public Policy/Administration with Health Policy concentration, Virginia Commonwealth University (2010).
profile.background: Raised in Whitcomb Court public housing in the East End; Whitcomb Elementary, Mosby Middle, Armstrong High School. Public-health / nonprofit career: Executive Director, National Black Women's Health Project (Atlanta, early 1990s); Head Start Program director at Charles Drew University (Compton, CA); Interim Co-director, Richmond City Public Health Department; Associate Director, VCU Grace E. Harris Leadership Institute; Senior Advisor, Richmond Memorial Health Foundation; Annie E. Casey Foundation consultant; Chief of Staff to Richmond City Manager. Co-founder of SisterFund (2015), giving circle for African-American women/girls. Sports Backers board member. Vegetarian. Lives near 27th & Nine Mile Road, East End. Richmond City Council, District 7 (East End), officially-nonpartisan (real-world Democratic alignment). First elected via special election 2009; current term Jan 2 2025 – Dec 31 2028 (re-elected Nov 5 2024 with 66.46% over Eric Sundberg's 32.83%). Council President 2019-2022, Vice President 2017-2018, Council President again 2025-2026 (third stint).

cynthia-newbille.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 7 (East End), current term 1/2/2025-12/31/2028, currently Council President 2025-2026 (3rd stint). Officially nonpartisan; real-world Democratic alignment. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 (CLEARS HIGH BAR via multi-vector affirmative pro-police record): (1) July 2020 amid George Floyd unrest she voted with majority REJECTING reductions to the RPD budget in response to defund-the-police demands (Richmond Free Press 7/30/2020, WRIC) — 'conditional no' on defund framework; (2) 2022 affirmatively proposed $1.9M-$4M in RPD and FIRE pay raises with VP Robertson and Trammell (multi-source) — that is advocacy, not routine governance; (3) Feb 2026 walked Shockoe Bottom businesses with Police Chief Rick Edwards after a mass shooting, quoted 'The goal is to share, to hear from the community, and to really map out strategies' — partnership/community-policing work beyond budget. Three independent vectors of affirmative pro-police advocacy clears the SKILL's HIGH BAR ('NOT just routine collaboration… affirmative advocacy: runs a police-partnership program, public pro-police statements'). This is a meaningful counter-archetype catch — a Democratic-aligned council president with a real pro-police record. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: as Council President co-signed the June 10 2025 Joint Proclamation 'Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community in perpetuity' with Mayor Avula and Council VP Jordan (rva.gov). Affirmative state-government LGBTQ promotion = direction-adverse. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: Oct 14-15 2025 co-sponsored with Mayor Avula the proposal to HOLD the real-estate tax rate steady at $1.20/$100, defeating Reva Trammell's proposed cut; direct Newbille quote: 'I look forward to when we will be able to reduce the tax rate. I do not see that in this moment.' (VPM 10/15/2025). Direct on-record opposition to tax-rate cut → adverse on rate-restraint cell. Voted YES on May 13 2025 6-2 adoption of the $3B FY26 budget keeping the $1.20 rate. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: May 24 2021 voted with unanimous council to declare gun violence a 'public health crisis' alongside Mayor Stoney (WTVR 5/13/2021, WRIC, RVA.gov GVPI page). Given her public-health PhD and decades of public-health-policy career, this is the most quintessential q3 fit on the council. HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q2: Sept 13 2021 voted with unanimous council to pass the Climate and Ecological Emergency Resolution and endorsed creation of a residential energy-efficiency program (Sierra Club Falls of the James, Richmonder energy-efficiency story) — the rubric's q2 cell specifically names ESG, and unanimous-council ESG-aligned action with her vote = direction-adverse. HARD FALSE border_immigration q2: as Council President co-signed admin statement 'Local government is not in charge of federal immigration law enforcement and Richmond does not detain or arrest anyone due to immigration status' (RVA.gov immigration response page); RPD under city policy has not signed a 287(g) agreement; Richmond appears/appeared on the federal sanctuary-jurisdictions list; in July 2025 when a federal agent accessed RPD Flock Safety camera data for immigration enforcement Newbille stayed publicly silent despite multiple media inquiries (Church Hill Lookout — HARD documentation of non-response). Anti-ICE-cooperation posture = adverse on q2's federal-preemption-against-sanctuary cell. HARD FALSE self_defense q0 + q1: July 1 2019 voted YES on the ordinance banning firearms in Richmond city buildings and parks; direct Newbille quote characterized it as 'one way to limit gun violence' and said council 'needed to act to help citizens feel safe on city property' (WTVR 7/1/2019); supported (with delay for refinement) Stoney's ordinance prohibiting firearms near permitted events. Richmond did NOT pass a Second Amendment Sanctuary resolution under her council presidency, in contrast to most VA rural localities in the 2019-2020 wave — directionally adverse to the constitutional-carry / oppose-restrictions cells. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / public_justice q1/q2/q4 / refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q3/q4 / border_immigration q1 — no quote-bearing positions located. NOT scored biblical_marriage q0/q2 — Pride proclamation cite scores q4 (LGBTQ promotion) directly but no marriage-definition or biological-sex quote → null per strict cite-only discipline. Counter-archetype context (CAPTURED in q0 TRUE): she is NOT a defunder. Her July 2020 vote with the majority rejecting police-budget cuts + her 2022 pay-raise proposal + her partnership work with Chief Edwards are real and meaningful softeners on the standard Democratic-progressive coding. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget vote and any 2026 ICE-policy or 2A council action.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of denominational affiliation or church membership. No public statement of faith in campaign materials, council bios, or news coverage. Wedding was at the Virginia Museum of Fine Arts — civil, not ecclesiastical — suggestive but not dispositive.
profile.education: B.A. University of Virginia (2004); graduate degree in economics, Duke University (2012).
profile.background: Born ~1982 in the D.C. area. Career: financial sector at BlackRock in New York City; left after the 2008 crisis ('witnessed banks bailed out while working families lost everything'); moved to Richmond 2008. Currently a financial data architect / data engineer. Wife is a physician (VCU Medical School); at least one child at Mary Munford Elementary (Breton served as PTA board member and fundraising chair). Prior civic work in Boston with Progressive Massachusetts (chapter chair, statewide board). Former CHAIR of the Richmond City Democratic Committee before running for council. Organized the June 7 2023 children's march on the Virginia State Capitol for gun-safety legislation following the Huguenot HS graduation shooting. Richmond City Council, District 1 (West End), officially-nonpartisan ballot, sworn 1/1/2025; term 1/1/2025 – 12/31/2028. Won Nov 5 2024 general 49.1% defeating Paul Goldman 27.7% and Zachary Walker 22.4%, succeeding Andreas Addison (who ran for mayor).

andrew-breton.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 1 (West End), 'Gumby' Breton, first term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028. Former chair of Richmond City Democratic Committee — real-world Democratic alignment on an officially-nonpartisan ballot. RPS public-school dad / former Mary Munford PTA fundraising chair (institutional ally of the public-school system). Most of his public posture is technocratic-progressive-urbanist (walkability, zoning reform / 'missing middle,' multimodal transit, RVAgreen 2050 climate plan, gas-utility phase-out, gun-safety) with DELIBERATE silence on culture-war markers (abortion, marriage, parental rights, religion). Per the SKILL's 'NO EVIDENCE FOUND beats inference' discipline, the absence of cited quotes on those markers is captured as null rather than inferred FALSE. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: Richmond Mayorathon candidate Q&A — 'Funding resources and interventions for those who are most at-risk to commit violence will reduce incidents' is textbook public-health / intervention framing of violent crime; direction-adverse on q3. HARD FALSE self_defense q0 + q1: organized the June 7 2023 children's march on the Virginia State Capitol for gun-safety legislation following the Huguenot HS graduation shooting (bretonforrichmond.com/issues; Daily Progress 6/8/2023). Moms Demand Action is listed as a campaign endorser (bretonforrichmond.com/endorsements). Multi-vector pro-gun-control posture is direction-adverse to the constitutional-carry / oppose-restrictions cells. Sept 8 2025 vote: supported activating the Civilian Review Board for RPD oversight, quote 'I'm glad to see that we can get the process started… many Richmonders will be glad to see that we're adding this additional layer of fair accountability to our justice system'; also said 'I believe it's very appropriate that we're also taking this moment to affirm Richmond's commitment to good policing now and moving forward' (WTVR 9/8/2025). NOT scored TRUE on public_justice q0 — CRB activation is a process / accountability measure, not affirmative pro-police advocacy, and Breton's 'good policing' language is supportive-of-accountability framing not affirmative thin-blue-line; doesn't clear the HIGH BAR. NOT scored FALSE on q0 either — he didn't vote to defund and his 'commitment to good policing' framing is supportive enough to leave null per the strict cite-only discipline. NOT scored economic_stewardship q2 — supports a rebate / tax-relief approach ('we have room to roll back the tax rate' at richmonder.org candidate forum), but his specific yea/nay on the May 12-13 2025 FY26 budget 6-2 vote was not confirmed in retrieved sources; also backed an independent salary study for city employees and is mostly silent on aggressive rate cuts; left null per cite-only discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / public_justice q1/q2/q4 / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration — no quote-bearing positions located despite extensive search of campaign site, city council records, candidate questionnaires, and news coverage. Sparse 3 FALSE / 0 TRUE on cited cells reflects his deliberate culture-war silence in public materials — honest report per the 'NO EVIDENCE FOUND beats inference' brand discipline. Counter-archetype context: civic-review-board support + public-health framing of crime + Moms Demand endorsement establish a clear progressive-urbanist coalition profile, but the absence of any pro-life, pro-traditional-marriage, pro-school-choice, or pro-religious-liberty quotes also documents the deliberate silence — neither inference is fair. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget vote, the June 10 2026 Pride proclamation cycle (will he sign or co-sponsor anything?), and any 2026 council action on sanctuary, abortion, or firearms.
